informationalTexts.title: Healthy Foods
informationalTexts.content:
Healthy foods give our bodies the nutrients they need to grow strong and stay well. Fruits and vegetables provide vitamins and minerals that help our immune system fight off sickness. Whole grains like brown rice and oatmeal give us energy that lasts a long time. Proteins from foods like fish, chicken, beans, and nuts help build and repair our muscles.
Eating a variety of healthy foods is important because different foods provide different nutrients that our bodies need. Dairy products like milk and cheese give us calcium for strong bones and teeth. Drinking plenty of water helps our bodies work properly and keeps us hydrated. When we eat healthy foods regularly and limit sugary snacks and drinks, we feel more energetic and are better able to learn and play.

questions.question: What do proteins help with in our bodies?
options: they help build and repair our muscles

questions.question: Why should we eat a variety of healthy foods?
options: because different foods provide different nutrients our bodies need

informationalTexts.title: Sleep
informationalTexts.content:
Sleep is a natural state of rest that our bodies and brains need every day to stay healthy and function properly. During sleep, our bodies repair themselves, grow new cells, and strengthen our immune system to fight off illnesses. Our brains use sleep time to organize memories from the day and prepare for learning new things tomorrow.
Children need more sleep than adults because their bodies and brains are still growing and developing. Most school-age children need about 9-11 hours of sleep each night to feel rested and alert during the day. Good sleep helps us concentrate better in school, have more energy for playing, and feel happier overall. Creating a bedtime routine and keeping a regular sleep schedule helps ensure we get the rest we need.

questions.question: What happens to our bodies during sleep?
options: our bodies repair themselves and strengthen our immune system

questions.question: How much sleep do school-age children need?

informationalTexts.title: Exercise
informationalTexts.content:
Exercise is physical activity that makes our bodies stronger, healthier, and more flexible. When we exercise, our hearts pump faster, our muscles work harder, and our breathing increases. Regular exercise helps build strong bones and muscles, improves our balance and coordination, and gives us more energy for daily activities.
There are many fun ways to exercise, like playing sports, riding bikes, swimming, dancing, or even playing active games at recess. Exercise also helps our brains work better by increasing blood flow and oxygen to help us think more clearly and concentrate in school. Additionally, physical activity can help us feel happier and less stressed by releasing natural chemicals in our brains that improve our mood.

questions.question: What happens to our bodies when we exercise?
options: our hearts pump faster and muscles work harder
we get tired and stay tired

questions.question: How does exercise help our brains?
options: it increases blood flow and oxygen to help us think clearly

informationalTexts.title: Music
informationalTexts.content:
Music is organized sounds that create rhythms, melodies, and harmonies that people enjoy listening to and creating. Music can be made with instruments like pianos, guitars, drums, and violins, or with the human voice through singing. Different cultures around the world have their own unique styles of music, and people have been making and enjoying music for thousands of years.
Learning music and playing instruments has many benefits for children. It helps develop listening skills, improves memory and concentration, and teaches patience and discipline through practice. Music also helps people express their emotions and creativity. Many schools have music programs where students can learn to play instruments, sing in choirs, or compose their own songs. Playing music with others teaches teamwork and cooperation.

questions.question: What can music be made with?
options: with instruments and the human voice

questions.question: What benefits does learning music provide?
options: it improves listening skills, memory, and concentration

informationalTexts.title: Recycling
informationalTexts.content:
Recycling is the process of collecting used materials and turning them into new products instead of throwing them away as trash. Common materials that can be recycled include paper, plastic bottles, aluminum cans, and glass jars. When we recycle these materials, they are cleaned, broken down, and made into new items, which helps reduce the amount of waste in landfills and conserves natural resources.
Recycling is important for protecting our environment because it reduces pollution and saves energy. Making new products from recycled materials uses less energy than making them from brand new materials. For example, making paper from recycled paper uses much less water and energy than making paper from trees. Everyone can help by separating recyclable materials from regular trash and putting them in special recycling bins.

questions.question: What materials can be recycled?
options: paper, plastic bottles, aluminum cans, and glass jars

questions.question: Why is recycling important for the environment?
options: it reduces pollution and saves energy

it creates more trash

**Key Points:**

• Practice using it correctly

**Practice:**

languageLessons.content:
Rhyming words sound the SAME at the end. They make poems and stories fun to read aloud!

**Rhyming Words:**
• cat, hat, mat, sat (all end with -at)
• play, day, say, way (all end with -ay)
• book, look, cook, took (all end with -ook)

**From today's story:**
Listen for words that rhyme as you read!

**Practice - Find the rhyme:**
1. Which word rhymes with "dog"? (log, big, cat)
2. Which word rhymes with "jump"? (run, bump, sit)
3. Which word rhymes with "bed"? (chair, red, sleep)

**Remember:** Listen to the END of words to find rhymes!
languageLessons.objective: Students will identify and create rhyming words.
